channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇸 Russia targets US firms in Ukraine as White House stays silent
item.description: Russian forces have deliberately struck facilities tied to Coca-Cola, Cargill, Mondelez and other American companies in Ukraine. The Trump administration's muted response to these attacks on US corporate interests has raised concerns about its commitment to protecting American assets abroad.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 Xi to press Trump on Taiwan arms sales at China summit
item.description: Beijing has called Taiwan the core of China's core interests, and Xi Jinping is expected to push President Trump to slow approval of weapons sales to the self-governing island during their high-stakes meeting in Beijing.

item.title: ⚖️🇺🇸 Supreme Court blocks Alabama congressional map with two Black districts
item.description: The US Supreme Court halted a lower court order requiring Alabama to draw a congressional map with two majority-Black districts, a significant intervention in the ongoing redistricting battles ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: 🇻🇪🇬🇾 Venezuela rejects Trump's 51st state remarks as territorial dispute escalates
item.description: Venezuela's acting president firmly rejected President Trump's comments suggesting the country could become a US state, defending the nation's territorial integrity amid ongoing tensions over the Essequibo region bordering Guyana.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚖️ Israeli parliament approves special tribunal with death penalty for October 7 attackers
item.description: Israeli lawmakers backed the creation of a special tribunal with authority to impose death sentences on those accused of involvement in the October 7 attacks, a controversial move that human rights groups have criticized.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky ex-chief of staff Yermak charged in corruption probe
item.description: Andriy Yermak, former chief of staff to President Zelensky and Ukraine's lead negotiator with the US, has been named as a suspect in a major money-laundering investigation tied to luxury development near Kyiv. Six people have been charged in the anti-corruption sweep.

item.title: 💰📈🛢️ US Inflation Surges to 3.8% as Iran War Drives Energy Prices Higher
item.description: The key US inflation measure rose to its highest level since May 2023 as consumers feel the direct impact of the Iran war on energy costs. The spike adds economic pressure on American households.

item.title: 🇺🇬🏛️ Museveni Sworn In for Seventh Term, Extending 40-Year Rule in Uganda
item.description: Uganda's President Yoweri Museveni, 81, was sworn in after winning disputed January elections, extending his four-decade grip on power in one of Africa's longest-serving leaders.

item.title: 🏥⚠️🦠 WHO Warns Countries to Prepare for More Hantavirus Cases
item.description: The WHO head said the situation could still change with more confirmed cases expected, as a cruise ship outbreak continues to raise concerns about the spread of the disease.

item.title: 🇮🇱 IDF forces deploy 10km deep into Lebanese territory
item.description: Israeli Defense Forces have crossed the Litani River and established positions 10 kilometers inside Lebanese territory, according to Haaretz. The deployment comes amid ongoing tensions with Hezbollah, though the militant group says disarmament is not part of Israel-Lebanon talks.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran redefines Strait of Hormuz boundaries 10 times wider
item.description: An IRGC officer says Iran now defines the Strait of Hormuz as 10 times wider than before the war, significantly expanding what Tehran considers its territorial waters. The move could escalate tensions with shipping in the critical waterway.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine drones hit Russian city 1,200km from front lines
item.description: Russian cities Orenburg and Chelyabinsk, located over 1,200 kilometers from Ukraine, were reportedly struck by drones after the Victory Day ceasefire ended. The attacks demonstrate Ukraine's expanding long-range strike capabilities deep into Russian territory.

item.title: 🇪🇸 Spain reports new hantavirus case as outbreak grows to 11
item.description: Spain's hantavirus outbreak has grown to 11 confirmed cases, with the WHO chief saying there is currently no sign of a larger outbreak. Health authorities are monitoring the situation closely as the rare viral disease spreads.

item.title: 🌍 Global Conflict Displacements Hit Record 32 Million in 2025
item.description: Internal displacements from violence and conflict reached an all-time high of 32.3 million people in 2025, surpassing disaster-driven displacements for the first time since tracking began in 2008.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines ICC Warrant Sparks Dramatic Senate Standoff
item.description: Former Philippine police chief Ronald dela Rosa, an ally of ex-president Duterte, took refuge in the senate building to avoid arrest on an ICC warrant over crimes against humanity in the drug war.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Prepares for High-Stakes Summit With Unpredictable Trump
item.description: China's President Xi Jinping hosts Trump in Beijing this week for a summit overshadowed by the Iran war, Taiwan tensions, and trade disputes, with Beijing aiming to project stability amid U.S. volatility.

item.title: 🇸🇪🇷🇺 Sweden Detains Two Suspects Over Allegedly Aiding High-Tech Shipments to Russia
item.description: Swedish authorities have detained two people on suspicion of violating international sanctions by helping Russia procure advanced products, marking an escalation in enforcement of export controls.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇷🇺 EU Rejects Putin's Proposal for Ex-German Leader Schroeder in Future Security Talks
item.description: EU ministers dismissed Russia's suggestion that former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder play a role in future security discussions, citing his close relationship with Putin.

item.title: ⛪🇮🇱 Israeli Soldiers Jailed for Desecration of Virgin Mary Statue in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli military jails soldiers after a viral photo showed an Israeli soldier posing with a Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon, holding a cigarette up to its mouth in a desecration incident.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Prime Minister Starmer Refuses to Resign Amid Growing Leadership Crisis
item.description: British PM Keir Starmer faces mounting pressure to step down but says he will not resign, in what media describe as the most serious political crisis of his tenure.

item.title: 🔒 Google Thwarts Zero-Day Attack Developed With AI
item.description: Google says it stopped a sophisticated zero-day hack that was likely developed with AI assistance, marking one of the first confirmed cases of AI-assisted cyberattacks in the wild.

item.title: 🎓 Canvas Learning Platform Reaches Deal With Hackers to Prevent Massive Data Leak
item.description: Instructure, which owns the Canvas learning platform used by thousands of schools, paid hackers to prevent the release of 3.65TB of student data after a massive breach.

item.title: 📺 Texas Sues Netflix for Spying on Users Including Children
item.description: Texas filed a lawsuit against Netflix alleging the streaming giant illegally collected data on users including children, amid growing scrutiny of platform surveillance practices.

item.title: 🌾 Former World Bank Chief Says China Should Stop Hoarding Food and Fertilizer
item.description: David Malpass criticized China strategic stockpiling of food and fertilizer, saying its claim to developing nation status was no longer credible.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 California Mayor Resigns After Pleading Guilty to Acting as Chinese Agent
item.description: Eileen Wang, mayor of Arcadia, California, agreed to plead guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government. She was accused of promoting pro-Beijing propaganda without notifying US authorities, including content denying Uyghur genocide.
